title: Spicy Creamy Argentinian Mozzarella Quesadilla
description: A spicy and creamy twist on the classic quesadilla, featuring mozzarella cheese and an emulsified sauce. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Diced chicken breast
ingredients.notes: Boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked or sweet paprika works well

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher or sea salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Fine or coarse black pepper is fine

instructions: Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the chorizo and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the emulsified sauce ingredients until smooth.
Add the sauce to the skillet and stir to combine with the chorizo and chicken.
Add the mozzarella cheese to the skillet and stir until melted and well combined with the sauce.
Meanwhile, heat two small non-stick skillets or griddles over medium heat.
Place one half of a large flour tortilla in each skillet and sprinkle with the chorizo and chicken mixture, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the tortillas in half to enclose the filling and cook until the tortillas are crispy and the cheese is melted, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot.
